extra.reasoning: rec_drug_a is set true only by r1, whose condition is exactly (seizure_history and qt_prolongation and renal_impairment and hypertension). rec_drug_b can be set true by r4 (priority 50, IF seizure_history) but is set false by r5 (priority 90) under the identical condition (seizure_history and qt_prolongation and renal_impairment and hypertension). Since r5 has higher priority (90 > 50) than r4, whenever rec_drug_a becomes true the very same condition triggers r5, which overrides r4 and forces rec_drug_b := false. Therefore the only reachable state with rec_drug_a=true has rec_drug_b=false. In every other input combination, r1's condition fails so rec_drug_a defaults to false, making the conjunction false regardless of rec_drug_b. Concretely, at the sole rec_drug_a=true point (seizure_history=T, qt_prolongation=T, renal_impairment=T, hypertension=T, hyperkalemia/elderly arbitrary): rec_drug_a=true, rec_drug_b=false (r5 priority 90 beats r4 priority 50). The mutual-exclusion property (never rec_drug_a and rec_drug_b jointly true) holds across the entire input space.
